hero.title: Hearth Code
hero.subtitle: HearthCode is a warm, low-glare VSCode theme system: code by hearthlight on blackboard dark and parchment light.
philosophy.label: HearthCode Philosophy
philosophy.01.title: Ember, not flame
philosophy.01.body: High-saturation syntax colors burn bright but exhaust your eyes. Hearth keeps all highlight colors at 40–60% saturation — warm like cooling coal, not blinding like fire.
philosophy.02.title: Neither black nor white
philosophy.02.body: Pure black backgrounds cause halation around white text, blurring edges. Hearth Dark (default) uses #23201c, Dark Soft uses #2a2723, and Hearth Light uses #efe6d8 for a warmer low-glare paper tone.
philosophy.03.title: Hierarchy as semantics
philosophy.03.body: Brightness maps directly to semantic weight: keywords are brightest, functions next, variables after, and comments deliberately fade into the background.
philosophy.04.title: Color temperature as comfort
philosophy.04.body: The entire palette is warm-toned, below 4500K. High blue-light content strains the retina. A warm coding environment is easier on your eyes over long sessions.
